project_context.project: EA_MOD / HUEY_P / EAFIX modular trading system
project_context.purpose: Single mined registry of legacy standards that remain active under the current project direction.
project_context.non_goal: This file does not preserve legacy documents wholesale and does not make stale prose authoritative.

authority_policy.activation_requirement: A rule becomes blocking only when its enforcement gate exists, has pass/fail criteria, and writes evidence.

decision_policy.fail_closed_default: If a mined rule cannot be classified safely, mark it needs_review instead of active.

source_file_index.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt

active_patterns.title: Legacy-to-active standards mining
active_patterns.description: Legacy standards are not merged wholesale. Each rule is extracted, classified, tested against current direction filters, assigned a disposition, and traced back to source files.

active_patterns.title: Active rule record
active_patterns.description: A standard is active only when it has a stable ID, domain, category, current-direction alignment, source traceability, enforcement path, evidence requirement, and pass/fail semantics.

active_patterns.title: Superseded legacy rule record
active_patterns.description: A legacy rule that conflicts with current identity, registry, module, or contract direction must be retained only as a superseded record with replacement guidance.

active_rules.title: Format Python with Black
active_rules.rule: All Python code must pass Black formatting with line length 88.

active_rules.title: Sort imports with isort
active_rules.rule: Python imports must be grouped as standard library, third party, then local application imports, with alphabetical sorting inside each group.

active_rules.title: Require strict typing
active_rules.rule: All Python function and method signatures, return values, and class attributes must be typed and must pass strict mypy checks.

active_rules.title: Require public API docstrings
active_rules.rule: All public modules, classes, functions, and methods must have Google-style docstrings suitable for generated documentation.

active_rules.title: No blocking calls in async code
active_rules.rule: I/O-bound Python functions should use async/await, and blocking calls such as time.sleep() or requests.get() must not appear inside async functions.

enforcement.notes: Use Semgrep rule to detect blocking calls inside async def.

active_rules.title: Use structured safe logging
active_rules.rule: Python services must use centralized structured logging and must not log credentials, API keys, passwords, or other secrets.

active_rules.title: Use specific exceptions
active_rules.rule: Python code must use specific custom exceptions for domain failures and must avoid broad generic exception handling when a narrower exception is possible.

enforcement.notes: Use lint and Semgrep patterns for broad except and project exception conventions.

active_rules.title: Python quality gates are blocking
active_rules.rule: Black, isort, mypy, lint, security scanning, and tests must run in pre-commit or CI and must block merge on failure.

active_rules.title: Use approved Verb-Noun functions
active_rules.rule: PowerShell functions must use PascalCase approved Verb-Noun naming and approved PowerShell verbs.

active_rules.title: Use CmdletBinding for non-trivial functions
active_rules.rule: Non-trivial PowerShell functions must use [CmdletBinding()] to support common parameters such as -Verbose, -Debug, and -ErrorAction.

active_rules.title: Use SupportsShouldProcess for changes
active_rules.rule: PowerShell functions that create, modify, delete, or otherwise change system state must support ShouldProcess, enabling -WhatIf and -Confirm.

active_rules.title: Return objects, not formatted strings
active_rules.rule: Reusable PowerShell functions must emit objects suitable for pipeline consumption and must not use Write-Host for output that must be captured or redirected.

active_rules.title: Type and validate parameters
active_rules.rule: PowerShell parameters must have explicit types and appropriate validation attributes such as ValidateNotNullOrEmpty, ValidateSet, ValidateRange, or ValidateScript.

active_rules.title: Promote risky errors to terminating errors
active_rules.rule: Risky PowerShell operations must use try/catch/finally and must promote required command failures with -ErrorAction Stop or controlled $ErrorActionPreference.

active_rules.title: No hardcoded secrets
active_rules.rule: PowerShell scripts must not hardcode credentials, API keys, passwords, or sensitive connection strings; automated credentials must come from approved secret storage.

active_rules.title: Validate and sanitize paths
active_rules.rule: PowerShell scripts must treat external inputs as untrusted, validate paths, and use Join-Path or approved path-resolution modules instead of unsafe concatenation.

active_rules.title: No hardcoded runtime configuration
active_rules.rule: PowerShell scripts must read runtime configuration from the approved project configuration source and must not hardcode server names, paths, ports, or URIs.

active_rules.title: Use standardized exit codes
active_rules.rule: PowerShell scripts used by automation must return standardized exit codes: 0 success, 1 general error, 2 invalid parameters/configuration, and 3 security/permission error.

active_rules.title: Test PowerShell with Pester
active_rules.rule: PowerShell modules and scripts must have Pester tests, with external dependencies mocked where needed.

active_rules.title: Use module manifests for reusable modules
active_rules.rule: Reusable PowerShell modules must have .psd1 manifests with explicit FunctionsToExport and required module metadata.

active_rules.title: Use deterministic MQL4 file order
active_rules.rule: MQL4 files must use deterministic ordering: #property directives, #include statements, #define constants, input parameters, globals, event handlers, then helper functions.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Use modern Build 600+ event handlers
active_rules.rule: MQL4 EAs must prefer OnInit, OnTick, OnDeinit, OnTimer, OnTrade, and other Build 600+ event handlers instead of legacy init/start/deinit as the canonical standard.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

enforcement.notes: Scanner should flag init(), start(), deinit() in new code unless explicitly grandfathered.

active_rules.title: Require include guards and header documentation
active_rules.rule: .mqh include files must use header guards and include a top comment block explaining purpose and provided functions.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Apply MQL4 naming conventions
active_rules.rule: MQL4 input parameters must use PascalCase, globals must use g_ plus camelCase, locals must use camelCase, functions must use PascalCase, and constants must use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: No raw magic numeric literals in trading calls
active_rules.rule: Numeric literals used for order magic numbers, error codes, retry counts, or fixed trading parameters must be defined as named constants or input/config values.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Check every trading function result
active_rules.rule: Every OrderSend, OrderClose, OrderModify, OrderDelete, and related trading function call must check the return value immediately and call GetLastError() on failure.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Use bounded retry for recoverable broker errors
active_rules.rule: Recoverable broker or trade-context errors must use bounded retry with short delay/backoff; infinite loops and aggressive retry loops are forbidden.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Keep OnTick lightweight
active_rules.rule: OnTick must remain lightweight; heavy calculations, file I/O, long loops, and periodic maintenance should be moved to OnTimer or offloaded services where practical.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Every EA order requires unique Magic Number
active_rules.rule: Every EA-managed order must use a unique configured Magic Number so automated trades can be distinguished from manual trades and other EAs.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Persist and reuse OrderSend ticket
active_rules.rule: The ticket returned by OrderSend is the authoritative order identifier and must be stored and used for later OrderSelect, OrderModify, OrderClose, or OrderDelete calls.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Perform final EA-side pre-trade checks
active_rules.rule: Before any trade request, the EA must verify spread limits, free margin, trading permission/session, symbol, and price sanity.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Normalize trading prices
active_rules.rule: Stop-loss, take-profit, entry, and pending order prices must be normalized with NormalizeDouble and the symbol Digits/Point rules before broker submission.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Use broker server time
active_rules.rule: All MQL4 time-based trading logic must use TimeCurrent server time, not TimeLocal local-machine time.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
source_files.notes: Used only where it modernizes lifecycle references to OnInit/OnTick style.

active_rules.title: Use RAII for owned resources
active_rules.rule: C++ code must tie sockets, handles, buffers, and other resources to object lifetime using RAII.

active_rules.title: No raw owned new/delete
active_rules.rule: Owned C++ resources must use std::unique_ptr or std::shared_ptr as appropriate; raw new/delete is forbidden for owned resources.

active_rules.title: Protect shared state with mutexes
active_rules.rule: Any C++ data shared between threads, including signal queues, must be protected with std::mutex and lock_guard/unique_lock or equivalent safe synchronization.

active_rules.title: Use MQL4-compatible DLL exports
active_rules.rule: Functions exported to MQL4 must use __declspec(dllexport), __stdcall, and extern "C" to avoid name mangling and calling-convention mismatches.

active_rules.title: Check Windows API calls and wrap handles
active_rules.rule: Every fallible Windows API or Winsock call must check its return value immediately, and handles/sockets must be wrapped so they are always closed.

active_rules.title: Do not let exceptions cross DLL boundary
active_rules.rule: C++ exceptions must not cross exported DLL functions into MQL4; exported functions must catch exceptions and return integer error codes or documented error values.

active_rules.title: Validate caller-provided buffers
active_rules.rule: DLL functions that write into MQL4-provided buffers must accept and validate buffer sizes before writing.

active_rules.title: No secrets in source or plain config
active_rules.rule: API keys, database credentials, broker credentials, and other secrets must not be stored in source code or plain configuration files; use environment variables or approved secret storage.

active_rules.title: Validate all external inputs
active_rules.rule: Signal fields, configuration values, file inputs, network payloads, and command parameters must be validated for schema, range, type, and plausible trading values before processing.

active_rules.title: Run with least privilege
active_rules.rule: MT4, Python services, Docker services, scripts, and scheduled tasks should run with the minimum permissions needed for their function.

enforcement.notes: Requires deployment policy and operational validation.

active_rules.title: Secure cross-machine transport
active_rules.rule: If communication crosses machine boundaries, TCP/HTTP communication must use TLS or an approved secure channel.

active_rules.title: Use pytest with mocked external dependencies
active_rules.rule: Python unit tests must use pytest and mock external dependencies such as network, database, filesystem side effects, and broker interfaces.

active_rules.title: Maintain minimum coverage threshold
active_rules.rule: Automated tests must enforce at least 80 percent coverage for covered Python service code unless a module-specific waiver exists.

active_rules.title: Test MQL4 public include functions through test EA scripts
active_rules.rule: MQL4 public .mqh functions must be testable by dedicated .mq4 test EAs/scripts using Assert-style helpers and logged pass/fail output.

active_rules.title: Use C++ unit tests for DLL internals
active_rules.rule: C++ DLL internal logic must be covered by a lightweight C++ test framework such as Google Test.

active_rules.title: Test Python to DLL bridge
active_rules.rule: Integration tests must validate that the Python BridgeService and C++ DLL/client exchange valid signals and errors according to contract.

active_rules.title: Test DLL to MQL4 bridge behavior
active_rules.rule: Integration tests must validate that MQL4-facing DLL functions handle valid data, invalid data, and error codes correctly using a mock Python or socket service.

active_rules.title: Use demo-account E2E tests only
active_rules.rule: Signal-to-trade lifecycle tests must run only on demo/simulated environments and must verify symbol, volume, SL/TP, Magic Number, and execution result capture.

active_rules.title: Do not use production data in tests
active_rules.rule: Tests must use static fixtures, generated data, sanitized data, or synthetic data; production trading data is forbidden unless explicitly sanitized and approved.

enforcement.notes: Requires fixture policy and review gate.

active_rules.title: Identical inputs produce identical outputs
active_rules.rule: All governed operations must be deterministic and idempotent: identical inputs and configuration snapshots must produce identical outputs or fail closed with evidence.

active_rules.title: Use versioned contracts as SSOT
active_rules.rule: Message formats, event schemas, configuration contracts, and process contracts must be defined in versioned canonical schemas or registries and validated before use.

active_rules.title: Fail closed on integrity errors
active_rules.rule: Services must suppress trading decisions or state changes when input integrity, contract validation, risk checks, or dependency health checks fail.

active_rules.title: Fallbacks must be explicit and audited
active_rules.rule: Fallback mechanisms must have a strict order, explicit activation conditions, and audit evidence; silent fallback is forbidden.

active_rules.title: Python RiskService is central pre-trade gatekeeper
active_rules.rule: Core pre-trade risk validation and lot sizing must be centralized in the Python RiskService; MQL4 may perform final broker-side sanity checks but must not duplicate or bypass core risk logic.

active_rules.title: ExecutionEngine owns OrderSend
active_rules.rule: ExecutionEngine.mqh or the approved execution module must be the sole MQL4 owner of OrderSend and common trade-error handling.

active_rules.title: Use UTC outside MT4 and server time inside MT4
active_rules.rule: Python/database timestamps must be ISO 8601 UTC; MQL4 trading-session logic must use broker server time through TimeCurrent.

active_rules.title: Use atomic writes for file fallback
active_rules.rule: File-based communication fallback must write to a temporary file and then atomically rename to the final path to prevent partial reads.

active_rules.title: Modules own their full context
active_rules.rule: Each module should own its code, tests, docs, schemas, configuration, and state artifacts in or through a module-centered boundary.

active_rules.title: Use registry/path indirection
active_rules.rule: Code, plans, and generated artifacts should use stable IDs, module/process bindings, path keys, or registries rather than hard-coded filesystem paths wherever practical.

active_gates.title: C++ DLL safety and interop gate

superseded_rules.legacy_rule: All Python script files must follow [PROJECT]_[TYPE]_[DESCRIPTION].py or HUEY_P_TYPE_DESCRIPTION.py naming.

source_files.file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt

superseded_rules.reason_superseded: Conflicts with the current numeric/registry identity direction and current inventory of numeric-ID-prefixed Python files.
superseded_rules.replacement_policy: Use current project identity policy: stable file IDs, registry/path indirection, and module/process bindings. Human-friendly names may remain descriptive after or around the stable ID when allowed by language/runtime constraints.

superseded_rules.legacy_rule: All PowerShell script files must follow [PROJECT]_[TYPE]_[DESCRIPTION].ps1.
superseded_rules.reason_superseded: Potential conflict with current stable identity and path abstraction policy. The naming convention may be used for generated human-readable scripts only if it does not conflict with registry identity.
superseded_rules.replacement_policy: Use registry-managed identity and module/path abstraction; allow descriptive script names only as a secondary convention.

superseded_rules.legacy_rule: Core MQL4 functions are init(), start(), and deinit().
superseded_rules.reason_superseded: Current MT4 Build 600+ style should use OnInit, OnTick, OnDeinit, OnTimer, and related event handlers as canonical lifecycle functions.
superseded_rules.replacement_policy: Use MQL4-LIFECYCLE-001 for new code. Existing legacy code may be grandfathered only with explicit migration status.

superseded_rules.legacy_rule: 06_API_Contract_Documentation.md is the single source of truth for all message formats.
superseded_rules.reason_superseded: Current direction favors versioned contract schemas and registry-backed SSOT rather than a single narrative Markdown API document.
superseded_rules.replacement_policy: Use ARCH-SSOT-001: message formats must be defined in versioned JSON schemas, event contracts, or canonical registries; Markdown can be generated documentation only.

legacy_reference_rules.source_file: Here are the completed technical sp.txt
legacy_reference_rules.reason_not_active: Contains generated-spec prose, placeholder citations, and duplicate rules. It is useful as source evidence but not suitable as executable authority.
legacy_reference_rules.use: Archive and mine only unique valid rule candidates.

legacy_reference_rules.source_file: # HUEY_P Trading System - Complete Beginner Development Roadmap
legacy_reference_rules.reason_not_active: Useful for learning and examples but not a current governance standard or active execution contract.

legacy_reference_rules.source_file: # HUEY_P Trading System - Complete Deployment Guide
legacy_reference_rules.reason_not_active: Deployment status and tool versions may be stale and must not drive current automation without verification.

needs_review.reason: Several high-value MQL4 rules are enforceable only with a custom scanner or strict code review until tooling exists.
needs_review.recommended_next_action: Create scanner rules for OrderSend ownership, unchecked trade calls, TimeLocal usage, NormalizeDouble usage, Magic Number presence, and raw numeric trading literals.

needs_review.reason: Legacy file naming rules conflict with current numeric/registry identity conventions. Python module import constraints may require a safe wrapper/facade strategy.
needs_review.recommended_next_action: Define final filename vs. registry-ID policy for Python, MQL4, PowerShell, C++, and generated artifacts.

needs_review.topic: Confirm RiskService vs. MQL4 responsibility boundary
needs_review.reason: The mined rule centralizes lot sizing and core risk in Python RiskService while allowing EA-side final checks. This boundary should be represented in module contracts.
needs_review.recommended_next_action: Bind RiskService responsibility to module_catalog and process_step_catalog contracts.

needs_review.reason: Legacy documents mention sockets, named pipes, files, DDE, REST, Redis, and CSV. Current architecture must state which hierarchy is authoritative per channel.
needs_review.recommended_next_action: Create per-channel fallback contracts for MT4↔Python price ingest, signal delivery, execution reports, and reentry decisions.
